text.title: django-quality Core Rules
text.summary: Five rules for the Django quality toolchain.

rule.statement: `ruff` replaces `black`, `isort`, `flake8`, `pyupgrade`, `pydocstyle`. There is no `setup.cfg`, no `.flake8`, no `pyproject.toml` `[tool.black]` block. Single source of truth: `[tool.ruff]`.
rule.rationale: ruff is 10-100x faster and consolidates the toolchain. Running multiple formatters concurrently produces conflicting fixes and slow pre-commit runs.

rule.statement: `mypy --strict` runs in CI with `plugins = ["mypy_django_plugin.main"]`. `django-stubs` and `djangorestframework-stubs` installed. Per-module overrides for `tests.*` and `migrations.*`.
rule.rationale: Without the django plugin, every `.objects.filter()` is `Any`. With it, the entire ORM is statically typed.

rule.statement: `bandit -r apps/ -ll` runs in CI, scoped to high-severity findings. Allowlist any findings explicitly in `.bandit` or `# nosec` comments with justification.
rule.rationale: Bandit catches `eval`, hard-coded secrets, weak crypto, shell injection. Low-severity findings are too noisy for a gate; high-severity only.

rule.statement: `pre-commit` runs ruff (format + check), mypy, bandit, djlint, and a `check-migrations` hook. Configuration in `.pre-commit-config.yaml`.
rule.rationale: Pre-commit centralises hooks across editors and CI. Without it, hooks drift between developer machines.

rule.statement: All tool config in `pyproject.toml`. No `setup.cfg`, no `.flake8`, no `.isort.cfg`, no `pytest.ini`. One file = one source of truth.
rule.rationale: Scattered config produces silent overrides and broken IDE pickup.
